description: Add missing HTTP endpoints and WebSocket protocol methods to the architecture and MCP server reference pages, and fix incorrect reconnection backoff description in troubleshooting.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add missing endpoints and WS methods to architecture page
userStories.description: Add /mcp/gateway, /tools, /tools/:name/call, and /plugin-settings to the HTTP endpoints table. Add config.setPluginSettings to the Extension → Server wire protocol table.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP endpoints table in architecture.mdx includes /mcp/gateway row with POST/GET/DELETE, Bearer token auth, description mentioning gateway MCP with 2 meta-tools
HTTP endpoints table includes /tools row with GET, Bearer token auth, description mentioning REST tool listing
HTTP endpoints table includes /tools/:name/call row with POST, Bearer token auth, description mentioning REST tool invocation
HTTP endpoints table includes /plugin-settings row with POST, Bearer token auth, description mentioning plugin settings for CLI
Extension → Server wire protocol table includes config.setPluginSettings row as Request type, description mentioning setting plugin configuration values
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx (add rows to HTTP endpoints table and wire protocol table)

Context:
The architecture page has an HTTP endpoints table (around line 20-73) that lists /mcp, /ws, /ws-info, /health, /audit, /reload, /extension/reload but is missing several newer endpoints. The Extension → Server wire protocol table (around line 246) lists many methods but is missing config.setPluginSettings.

Changes:
1. In the HTTP endpoints table, add these rows (in a logical position near related endpoints):
   - /mcp/gateway | POST/GET/DELETE | Bearer token | Gateway MCP endpoint — exposes 2 meta-tools (opentabs_list_tools, opentabs_call) for minimal context cost.
   - /tools | GET | Bearer token | REST tool listing — used by `opentabs tool list`. Supports `?plugin=` filter.
   - /tools/:name/call | POST | Bearer token | REST tool invocation — used by `opentabs tool call`.
   - /plugin-settings | POST | Bearer token | Plugin settings update — used by `opentabs plugin configure`.
2. In the Extension → Server wire protocol table, add a row:
   - config.setPluginSettings | Request | Set plugin configuration values from the side panel.

Constraints:
- Keep the existing table formatting style (Table.Row, Table.Cell with className patterns).
- Place /mcp/gateway right after /mcp in the table.
- Place /tools and /tools/:name/call together, after the existing admin endpoints.
- Place /plugin-settings near /reload.

userStories.title: Add missing endpoint and WS method to MCP server reference page
userStories.description: Add /plugin-settings to the HTTP endpoints table and config.setPluginSettings to the Extension → Server WebSocket protocol table in the MCP server reference.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP endpoints table in mcp-server.mdx includes /plugin-settings row with POST, Bearer token + rate-limited auth, description mentioning plugin settings update for CLI
Extension → Server WebSocket protocol table includes config.setPluginSettings row as Request type
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx (add rows to HTTP endpoints table and WS protocol table)

Context:
The MCP server reference page has an HTTP endpoints table (around line 101-176) and Extension → Server WebSocket protocol tables (around line 549-645). The /plugin-settings endpoint is defined in platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts (line 603: `Plugin settings endpoint (POST /plugin-settings) — used by the CLI`). The config.setPluginSettings JSON-RPC method is routed in extension-protocol.ts.

Changes:
1. In the HTTP endpoints table, add a row for /plugin-settings:
   - /plugin-settings | POST | Bearer token, rate-limited | Plugin settings update — accepts `{ plugin, settings }`, validates against configSchema. Used by `opentabs plugin configure`.
   Place it near /reload (before or after).
2. In the Extension → Server table, add a row for config.setPluginSettings:
   - config.setPluginSettings | Request | Set plugin configuration values (plugin name + settings map).
   Place it near the other config.* methods.

Constraints:
- Match the existing Table component formatting exactly.
- Do NOT modify any existing table rows.

userStories.title: Fix incorrect WebSocket reconnection backoff description in troubleshooting
userStories.description: Fix the reconnection backoff description to match the actual source code constants: 1s initial, 2x multiplier, 3s max (not 30s).
acceptanceCriteria: Troubleshooting page reconnection section says backoff is 1s, 2s, 3s with a 3-second cap (not 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 30s)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx (fix backoff description)

Context:
The source code in platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts defines:
- INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS = 1000 (line 32)
- MAX_BACKOFF_MS = 3000 (line 33)
- BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER = 2 (line 34)
So actual backoff is: 1s → 2s → 3s (capped at 3s). The troubleshooting page currently says:
'The extension uses exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, up to a 30-second cap.'
This is wrong — the max is 3s, not 30s, and it only goes 1s → 2s → 3s before capping.

Changes:
In docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx, find the line:
`The extension uses exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, up to a 30-second cap.`
Replace with:
`The extension uses exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, then capped at 3 seconds. Backoff resets after a successful connection.`

Constraints:
- Only change the backoff description text. Do not modify the surrounding content.
